name: Quintanilla de la Mata, Iglesiarrubia y Paúles del Agua — circular desde Lerma
region_intro: Mountain biking around Lerma offers diverse terrain within the province of Burgos, Spain. The region is characterized by varied landscapes, including the rounded mountain summits and dense forests of the Sierra de la Demanda, and the ancient juniper forests and rocky terrain of the Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Park. Riders can expect routes that follow river valleys, traverse open plains, and climb through forested areas, providing a range of elevations and surfaces. This area provides a natural setting for mountain biking, with trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Lerma The most popular mountain bike trail is Quintanilla de la Mata, Iglesiarrubia y Paúles del Agua — circular desde Lerma, a 19.6 miles (31.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the rural landscapes surrounding Lerma. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Patrimonio de la Luz Conjunto Monumental — circular desde Lerma por Hontoria de la Cantera, a moderate 36.6 miles (58.9 km) path. This route features varied terrain and passes through areas with historical significance and natural beauty. Local mountain bikers also love the Burgos Cathedral – Burgos Cathedral loop from Madrigalejo del Monte, a 84.4 miles (135.9 km) trail leading through the broader region, often completed in about 11 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Lerma is defined by ancient juniper forests, Arlanza River valleys, and varied elevations within the Sierra de la Demanda. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Lerma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 140 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lerma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Lerma?
A: The terrain around Lerma is quite diverse. You'll find routes traversing the rounded mountain summits and dense forests of the Sierra de la Demanda, as well as the ancient juniper forests and rocky landscapes of the Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Park. Expect a mix of river valleys, open plains, and forested climbs, offering varied elevations and surfaces.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Lerma?
A: Yes, Lerma offers several trails suitable for beginners. There are 2 easy mountain bike routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10462221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Covarrubias – Statue of El Cid in Mecerreyes loop from Tordueles&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 30 km trail that provides a pleasant ride through the region.

Q: What are some of the natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking near Lerma?
A: Mountain biking around Lerma allows you to explore significant natural features. The Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Park is home to ancient juniper forests and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find impressive viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5517941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Arcos&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of Lerma and the Arlanza River.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in the Lerma area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Lerma are circular. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100200473/quintanilla-de-la-mata-iglesiarrubia-y-paules-del-agua-circular-desde-lerma" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintanilla de la Mata, Iglesiarrubia y Paúles del Agua — circular desde Lerma&lt;/a&gt;, which is 31.5 km long and explores the rural landscapes. Another notable circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104394483/patrimonio-de-la-luz-conjunto-monumental-circular-desde-lerma-por-hontoria-de-la-cantera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Patrimonio de la Luz Conjunto Monumental — circular desde Lerma por Hontoria de la Cantera&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 58.9 km path featuring varied terrain and historical sites.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Lerma?
A: The mountain bike trails in Lerma are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars from over 5 reviews. Many riders appreciate the diverse landscapes, from ancient juniper forests to river valleys, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass through historical sites or cultural landmarks near Lerma?
A: Absolutely. The region around Lerma is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104394483/patrimonio-de-la-luz-conjunto-monumental-circular-desde-lerma-por-hontoria-de-la-cantera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Patrimonio de la Luz Conjunto Monumental — circular desde Lerma por Hontoria de la Cantera&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, passes through areas with historical significance. You can also find routes that lead near the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5698252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor and Ducal Palace of Lerma&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5698240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Saint Peter the Apostle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos Cathedral – Burgos Cathedral loop from Madrigalejo del Monte&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 135.9 km trail that typically takes around 11 hours to complete, leading through the broader region with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Lerma?
A: Lerma's diverse landscapes make it suitable for mountain biking across various seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While the Sierra de la Demanda features high mountain summits, many exceeding 2,000 meters, the lower elevation trails can be enjoyed for much of the year. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any specific areas known for wildlife viewing while mountain biking?
A: The Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Park is particularly known for its ecological diversity, including a large population of raptors due to its rocky terrain. The dense forests of pines, birch, beech, and poplar in the valleys of the Sierra de la Demanda also provide habitats for various wildlife, offering opportunities for observation during your ride.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in total around Lerma?
A: There are 27 mountain bike trails documented around Lerma, catering to a range of skill levels from easy to difficult. This includes 2 easy, 23 moderate, and 2 difficult routes, ensuring options for nearly every rider.

Q: Can I find routes that follow river valleys in the Lerma area?
A: Yes, the Arlanza River flows through the Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Park, and many routes in the region incorporate river valleys. These areas often feature riverside forests and offer a different riding experience compared to the higher mountain terrain.
tips: Surrounded by imposing Renaissance buildings, this square is a meeting point and center of activity in the town. Its arcades, balconies and stone arches give it a unique atmosphere. With its terraces and shops, it invites you to enjoy local life and immerse yourself in the atmosphere of this beautiful town.
An old town that is now part of Avellanosa de Muñó and where it is known for its tower, which rises above the rest of the hills with a spectacular floor plan.

Temple with a baroque-style façade consecrated in 1617. An authentic masterpiece that will take your breath away. Its imposing façade and its magnificently ornamented interior stand out, as well as its high vaults, chapels and golden altarpieces.
